question: Looking at the US GDP map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: The GDP differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.
options.B: All the highlighted states have nearly identical GDP levels.
options.C: The GDP gap between Utah and Arizona might exceed that between New Hampshire and New Jersey.
options.D: Utah and Arizona have similar GDPs.

explanation: Utah (189.7$B) and Arizona (245.5$B) have a 55.8$B GDP difference, while New Hampshire (39.8$B) and New Jersey (40.4$B) have only a 0.6$B difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Utah and Arizona into the same color bin (180-350$B range), making their large difference appear similar, while New Hampshire and New Jersey appear more different due to different color bins.
